articles.title: HDSC MCU Selection Guide
articles.summary: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right HDSC MCU for your application based on performance, power, and peripheral requirements.
articles.content:
Selecting the right microcontroller is critical for project success. This guide walks through the HDSC MCU portfolio and provides selection criteria based on application requirements.

## Ultra-Low Power Applications

For battery-powered IoT, wearables, and metering applications, the HC32L series offers exceptional power efficiency:

- HC32L110: Entry-level ultra-low power in small packages
- HC32L136: Mainstream ultra-low power with integrated LCD driver
- HC32L176: Enhanced features with more memory and peripherals
- HC32L196: Maximum performance in ultra-low power series

Key specifications to consider:
- Standby current: 0.5μA with RTC
- Active current: 35μA/MHz
- Wake-up time: 4μs from deep sleep

## High-Performance Applications

For industrial control, motor control, and complex applications:

- HC32F460: 200MHz Cortex-M4 with rich analog peripherals
- HC32F4A0: 240MHz high-performance with Ethernet and advanced timers

## Motor Control Applications

Dedicated motor control MCUs:

- HC32M423: Integrated gate drivers for BLDC/PMSM
- HC32F460: High-performance FOC control

## Selection Checklist

1. Define processing requirements
2. Determine memory requirements
3. List required peripherals and interfaces
4. Specify power consumption constraints
5. Consider package size and pin count
6. Evaluate temperature range requirements
7. Check for automotive qualification needs

Contact LiTong's application engineering team for personalized selection assistance.

articles.title: Ultra-Low Power Design with HDSC MCUs
articles.summary: Techniques and best practices for achieving minimum power consumption in battery-powered applications using HDSC HC32L series.
articles.content:
This application note provides detailed guidance on optimizing power consumption for battery-powered applications using HDSC HC32L series ultra-low power MCUs.

## Understanding Power Modes

The HC32L series offers multiple power modes to balance functionality with power consumption:

### Active Mode
- CPU running at full speed
- All peripherals available
- Current: ~100μA/MHz typical

### Sleep Mode
- CPU clock stopped
- Peripherals continue operating
- Fast wake-up for interrupts
- Current: ~30μA/MHz

### Deep Sleep Mode
- Main clock stopped
- LPO running, RTC active
- RAM retained
- Current: 0.5μA with RTC

### Shutdown Mode
- All clocks stopped
- Only wakeup pin active
- Current: 0.15μA

## Power Optimization Techniques

### 1. Clock Gating
Disable clocks to unused peripherals to save power.

### 2. GPIO Configuration
Unused pins should be configured to minimize leakage.

### 3. Peripheral Management
- Disable ADC between conversions
- Use DMA instead of CPU polling
- Lower UART baud rate when possible

### 4. Efficient Wake-up Strategy
- Use RTC for periodic wake-up
- Configure appropriate wake-up sources
- Minimize time in active mode

## Measuring Power Consumption

Accurate measurement requires:
1. Use high-precision multimeter
2. Measure current in series
3. Account for regulator efficiency
4. Consider temperature effects

LiTong provides a power calculator tool.

articles.title: Motor Control Fundamentals with HDSC MCUs
articles.summary: Introduction to motor control concepts and implementation using HDSC motor control MCUs and libraries.
articles.content:
This technical guide introduces motor control fundamentals and implementation using HDSC motor control MCUs.

## Motor Types Overview

### Brushless DC (BLDC) Motors
- Trapezoidal back-EMF
- 6-step commutation
- Simple control algorithm
- Good for high-speed applications

### Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSM)
- Sinusoidal back-EMF
- Field Oriented Control (FOC)
- Higher efficiency
- Better for variable speed/torque

## Control Algorithms

### Trapezoidal Commutation
Simplest BLDC control method with six switching states per cycle.

### Field Oriented Control (FOC)
Advanced control for PMSM with independent torque and flux control.

## HDSC Motor Control Features

### Hardware Features
- High-resolution PWM (1ns resolution)
- Integrated gate drivers
- Advanced timers with fault protection
- Fast ADC for current sensing

### Software Libraries
HDSC provides comprehensive motor control libraries:
- Sensorless FOC for PMSM
- Trapezoidal control for BLDC
- Parameter identification tools
- Auto-tuning utilities

## Implementation Steps

1. Hardware Setup
2. Parameter Identification
3. Control Loop Tuning
4. Testing and Validation

LiTong's Motor Control Workbench tool automates parameter identification.

articles.title: HDSC Development Tools and Software
articles.summary: Overview of HDSC development ecosystem including IDEs, debuggers, libraries, and evaluation boards.
articles.content:
HDSC provides a comprehensive development ecosystem to accelerate your design.

## Integrated Development Environments

### IAR Embedded Workbench
- Industry-standard compiler
- Excellent optimization
- Full HDSC device support

### Keil MDK
- ARM compiler
- Free version up to 32KB

### HDSC Studio
- Free Eclipse-based IDE
- GCC toolchain

## Debug Tools

### HDSC-Link
- Low-cost debugger/programmer
- SWD interface

### Third-party Debuggers
- J-Link, ULINK supported

## Software Libraries

### HDSC SPL and HAL
- Register-level and high-level APIs
- Example projects included

### Motor Control Library
- FOC and trapezoidal control
- Auto-tuning features

## Evaluation Boards

### HC32L196-EVAL
- Full-featured evaluation
- LCD display

### HC32F460-EVAL
- High-performance evaluation
- Ethernet and motor headers

LiTong provides technical support for tool setup.

articles.title: HDSC Motor Control Development Guide
articles.summary: Comprehensive guide for motor control application development using HDSC HC32F series MCUs.
content: This guide covers motor control application development using HDSC HC32F series microcontrollers with a focus on BLDC and PMSM motor control.
Brushless DC (BLDC) motors use electronic commutation:
1. **Motor vibration** - Check PWM frequency and dead-time
HDSC HC32F series provides excellent platforms for motor control applications with comprehensive libraries and development tools.

faeInsights.content: Motor control is all about timing. The HC32F4A0's advanced timers with dead-time insertion are excellent for motor applications. Start with the HDSC motor control library - it provides working examples for both BLDC and PMSM. For FOC applications, the triple ADC allows simultaneous sampling of all three phases. Always verify your current sensing circuit - accurate current measurement is critical for stable FOC operation.
keyTakeaways: Use HDSC motor control library as starting point
Verify current sensing accuracy
Triple ADC enables simultaneous phase sampling
Proper dead-time prevents shoot-through
Test with oscilloscope before connecting motor

faqs.question: Which MCU is best for FOC motor control?
faqs.answer: The HC32F4A0 is recommended for FOC with its 240MHz performance, FPU, and triple ADC for simultaneous current sampling. The HC32F460 works for simpler applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use HC32F4A0 for high-performance FOC, HC32F460 for basic BLDC.

faqs.question: Does HDSC provide motor control software?
faqs.answer: Yes, HDSC provides a motor control library with BLDC six-step and PMSM FOC algorithms, including sensorless estimators and auto-tuning features.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with HDSC motor control library for fastest development.

articles.title: HDSC MCU Frequently Asked Questions
articles.summary: Common questions and answers about HDSC MCUs, tools, and support.
articles.content:
## General Questions

Q: What is HDSC?
A: HDSC is a leading Chinese MCU manufacturer offering ARM Cortex-M based microcontrollers.

Q: Where can I buy HDSC MCUs?
A: LiTong is an authorized distributor of HDSC products.

Q: Are HDSC MCUs compatible with other ARM MCUs?
A: Yes, they use standard ARM Cortex-M cores.

## Technical Questions

Q: What debug interfaces are supported?
A: SWD (Serial Wire Debug) interface.

Q: What is the maximum clock speed?
A: HC32L: 48MHz, HC32F460: 200MHz, HC32F4A0: 240MHz

## Power Questions

Q: What is the lowest power mode current?
A: Deep sleep: 0.5μA, Shutdown: 0.15μA

Q: Can I use a coin cell battery?
A: Yes, HDSC ultra-low power MCUs are ideal for coin cell applications.

## Support Questions

Q: Where can I get technical support?
A: LiTong provides local technical support for HDSC products.

faqs.question: How do I get started with HDSC MCUs?
faqs.answer: Getting started with HDSC MCUs: (1) Select appropriate MCU based on application requirements using our selection guide; (2) Obtain evaluation board for your chosen MCU; (3) Download and install HDSC Studio IDE or use IAR/Keil; (4) Review example projects and documentation; (5) Connect debugger and run first program; (6) Contact LiTong FAE for technical support if needed. We provide comprehensive startup support including training and reference designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with evaluation board and example projects. Contact FAE for support.

faqs.question: What technical support does LiTong provide?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive technical support for HDSC products: (1) Application engineering - MCU selection, schematic review, debugging assistance; (2) Reference designs - complete hardware designs with documentation; (3) Software support - libraries, example code, troubleshooting; (4) Training - technical seminars and workshops; (5) Documentation - application notes, user manuals, quick start guides; (6) Long-term support - product lifecycle support and supply guarantee. Contact our FAE team for personalized assistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: LiTong provides end-to-end support from design to production.

faqs.question: Where can I find HDSC documentation?
faqs.answer: HDSC documentation resources: (1) Official website - datasheets, reference manuals, user guides; (2) LiTong website - application notes, selection guides, FAQs; (3) Development tools - HDSC Studio includes documentation; (4) Support articles - detailed technical articles on this website; (5) Reference designs - schematics, BOMs, design guides; (6) Training materials - presentation slides and videos. All documentation is available in English and Chinese. Contact LiTong for specific document requests.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check LiTong website and HDSC official site for complete documentation.

faqs.question: How do I report issues or request features?
faqs.answer: Reporting issues and feature requests: (1) Technical issues - contact LiTong FAE with detailed description and steps to reproduce; (2) Documentation errors - report to FAE or through website feedback; (3) Feature requests - submit to LiTong product team; (4) Bug reports - include MCU model, software version, and test code; (5) Support tickets - email or phone support for urgent issues. LiTong coordinates with HDSC for product improvements and issue resolution.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong FAE for all technical issues and requests.
